details.text: Derivative: ظُلَلٌ means canopies and as such it can be understood here to refer to layers, or layer upon layer.

Derivative: اتَّقُونِ from اِتَّقَى. It differs from خَافَ to fear and be afraid, in that it means to take caution to protect yourself. اِتَّقَى calls for proactive steps to protect oneself, whilst fear is just a reaction.

Derivative: الطَّاغُوتَ – It has some specific qualities including that it refers to a living entity that can communicate with humans (and so it does not include idols). الطَّاغُوتَ comes from طَغَى which means to rebel. الطَّاغُوتَ is an entity that calls or pulls people to want to rebel against and disobey Allah.
Phrase حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ - حَقَّ عَلَى means “to be deserved upon” or that it has become mandated, due or incumbent upon i.e., it has already become a fact, been determined and confirmed.
Derivative: مُخْلِصًا means purely and sincerely. It means to have إِخْلاص which is pure sincerity.

Derivative: ٱلدِّينَ literally refers to a judgement. Here it refers to your way of living and all judgements or decisions you make in life i.e., that we are commanded to be sincere in our worship to Allah in respect to this ٱلدِّينَ (our entire way of life).
- بِالْحَقِّ – This can be “With the truth (in the case where it is not blemished by falsehood)”, “In truth” and “with purpose”.
Derivative: غُرَفٌ refers to large and tall rooms or dwellings.

- شَرْحُ الصَّدْر means the opening or expanding of the chest and it is an expression that implies that the person’s chest is opened, expanded and consequently made ready to receive and accept something. Here Allah is opening the person’s chest to Islam. It implies that the person whose chest has been expanded is comfortable and at ease without any internal obstacles in the way of him accepting the truth and following it. It can also imply that he would only find comfort and peace of mind in Islam.

Derivative: مَّثَانِيَ – It contains matters of high praise from أَثْنَى (to praise and extol). Or it refers to pairs in the case where Allah discusses multiple matters in pairs or twos which is derived from ثَنَّى. Examples include, the promise of reward and the threat of punishment, the command and the forbiddance, the news (i.e., of former nations and Prophets) and the rulings etc.

Derivative: عِوَجٍ means crookedness, bendiness or twistedness. The absence of عِوَجٍ means that it is straight. Here it refers to the unambiguous and clear speech of the Qur’an that has no bends and twists in it, rather, it is clear, direct and unambiguous in its language and meanings.
Derivative: مُتَشَاكِسُونَ from تَشَاكَسَ. It means to dispute with one another and to employ bad mannerisms in that disputing and quarreling.
Derivative: كَفَّارٌ means excessively or extremely ungrateful.
Derivative: زُلْفَىٰ – It means closeness to Allah in terms of status and rank.
Derivative: مَثْوًى is a place that you reside in. It can imply a place that you reside in for a long time, generation after generation.

- “The one who possesses (ذِي) vengeance or retribution (انتِقَامٍ)” refers to the capability of dealing retribution which is different in implication to المُنْتَقِم (The one who avenges or deals retribution).
Derivative: حَسْبِ refers to sufficiency and something mean enough.
Derivative: مُّقِيمٌ implies permanency, staying, residing and abiding in a place continuously.
Derivative: يَحِلُّ with عَلَى means to descend upon (i.e., a punishment), to befall of unravel upon i.e., come to be or pass.

Derivative: الْأَنفُسَ is the جَمْعُ القِلَّةِ (Plural of few) of نَفْس (soul) whilst نُفُوس is the جَمْعُ الكَثْرَة (Plural of many). This use of language indicates that the human souls are not taken in death altogether in one go but rather it is ongoing process where the souls of some of the people are taken.
Derivative: اشْمَأَزَّتْ from اشْمَأَزَّ which means to recoil and feal nauseated.
Derivative: يَحْتَسِبُونَ from اِحْتَسَبَ which means to anticipate, expect, think of or imagine i.e., what they will be shown in the hereafter is beyond what they can imagine or expect.

Derivative: مُعْجِزِينَ comes from أَعْجَزَ – إِعْجاز which means to incapacitate something, nullify it or cancel it out. Here, it carries the meaning of not being able to escape, find a way out from or nullify the consequences of their bad deeds.
- To go over or transgress the limits (أَسْرَفُوا) against (عَلَىٰ) their own selves (أَنفُسِهِمْ) i.e., by committing sinful acts.
Derivative: أَنِيبُوا from أَنَابَ – إِنَابَة which means to return to Allah (in repentance after having gone astray or perpetrating sins).
Derivative: حَسْرَة refers to the feeling of regret.
Derivative: السَّاخِرِينَ from سَخِرَ which means to make fun, mock and scoff.
- Can mean “in relation to Allah in terms of His right and the obedience and submission due to Him”. Or it can refer to the company and continuous presence of Allah.
- يَا حَسْرَتَىٰ is an expression of regret, like saying: “Oh no, what have I done!” or simply “What great regret I have!”.
Derivative: تُصْرَفُونَ from صَرَفَ means to be averted and turned away, from one direction to another, or one state to another i.e., away from the truth.
Derivative: مَثْوًى from ثَوَى which means to reside in a place and settle in it. مَثْوًى is the place that one resides and settles in.

Derivative: مَفَازَة means فَوْز (attainment of success i.e., Al-Jannah) or it refers to the place of their فَوْز (attainment of success) i.e., Al-Jannah. It can also refer to the success in respect to attaining the pleasure and acceptance of Allah. According to the latter meaning, the verse would mean “Allah saves those who have Taqwa due to their success in attaining the pleasure of Allah and His acceptance”.

Derivative: مَقَالِيدُ is the plural of مِقْلاد and it is a key to a vault or safe filled with treasure. قَلَدَ carries the meaning of gathering something in a place and مِقْلاد refers to the device that holds what is gathered. مَقَالِيدُ in this context refers to the keys to the treasures of the heavens and earth.

Derivative: يَمِين means the right (i.e., direction). It also refers to the right hand which is additionally a metaphor for power. His يَمِين means his right hand or his power, ownership and authority.
Derivative: تَعَالَىٰ – To be high and far above.
Derivative: صَعِقَ means to tip over, fall down unconscious or senseless. Here, it means to pass out or to fall down dead.
Derivative: يَفْعَلُونَ from فَعَلَ – فِعْلٌ refers to both those actions which are consciously undertaken and those which are not, unlike عَمِلَ – عَمَلٌ which refers to the conscious actions.
Derivative: زُمَرًا – Refers to groups upon groups. Groups organised by category are called زُمَرًا i.e., that group after group will be driven toward Jahannam (hell).
Derivative: سِيقَ from سَاقَ which means to herd and drive i.e., towards a particular direction. It is used for cattle and prisoners.
Derivative: طِبْتُمْ from طَابَ which means to be happy or pleased, to be good and to be purified.

- حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءُوهَا وَفُتِحَتْ أَبْوَابُهَا – The وَاو (waw) before فُتِحَتْ is significant in respect to the difference between the meaning of the statement in this verse which discusses the believers being herded towards Jannah (paradise) and verse 71 which states حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءُوهَا فُتِحَتْ أَبْوَابُهَا and discusses the disbelievers being herded to Jahannam (hell). The وَاو (waw) which is “واوُ الحال” implies that the doors have already been opened before the believers reach it, whilst its absence in the previous verse indicates that the doors are only opened when they reach it. Consequently, the original status of the doors of hell is that they are closed when the disbelievers approach, whilst the original state of the doors of paradise is that they are open when the believers reach it. The former are being imprisoned and the latter are being welcomed.
Derivative: حَافِّينَ from حَفَّ means to surround and encircle.

Derivative: خَوَّلَ means to grant and confer (a blessing), to grant preference in the giving of something, to grant someone the possession of something and to give one time after another or one thing on top of another. (Ustadh Nu’man provides the meaning for خَوَّلَ as being wrapped up in something, here to be wrapped up in blessings, layers upon layers (however I cannot find any reference for this meaning in the dictionaries or Tafasir).
Derivative: قَانِتٌ refers to the one who is ready and enthusiastic to obey. The one who is devoutly and sincerely obedient.

أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَانِتٌ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ سَاجِدًا وَقَائِمًا يَحْذَرُ الْآخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُو رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِ

- “Is one who is devoutly obedient during periods of the night, prostrating and standing [in prayer], fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord …”. This statement is left incomplete, utilising a style where the one who reads can deduct what has been omitted. Here, the meaning of the omission could be: “Like the one who is not like that” or “Like the one who does not do that”.
